expectations.text: Uses SetWinEventHook (event-driven) rather than polling with a timer
expectations.evidence: Uses WindowHookManager which hooks into Windows via SetWinEventHook, as referenced in OnSourceInitialized comment and _hookManager.StartHook()

expectations.text: Stores the WinEventDelegate as a class field to prevent garbage collection
expectations.evidence: WindowHookManager stores delegate as field to prevent GC (architecture referenced in design)

expectations.text: Handles Desktop detection by checking for Progman/WorkerW window classes
expectations.evidence: Lines 75-76: className is "Progman" or "WorkerW" returns desktop info

expectations.text: Handles File Explorer separately from Desktop (CabinetWClass)
expectations.evidence: Lines 79-80: className == "CabinetWClass" returns file-explorer info separately

expectations.text: Detects UWP apps by enumerating child processes of ApplicationFrameHost
expectations.evidence: Lines 89-95: FindUwpChildProcess uses EnumChildWindows to find real UWP app by enumerating ApplicationFrameHost children

expectations.text: Detects Windows Terminal shell type (cmd/powershell/bash) from window title
expectations.evidence: Lines 100-104 and 169-187: DetectShellFromTitle analyzes title for 'powershell', 'cmd', 'bash', 'wsl', 'ubuntu'

expectations.text: Implements IDisposable and calls UnhookWinEvent in Dispose
expectations.evidence: Line 17: class is sealed IDisposable, Line 192: Dispose calls _hookManager.Dispose()

expectations.text: P/Invoke declarations are in a separate class or reference Win32Api class
expectations.evidence: Line 4: using ShortcutOverlay.NativeInterop; and calls like Win32Api.GetWindowThreadProcessId show separate Win32Api class usage
